mcqs.question: Which of the following is a core component responsible for converting a physical phenomenon into an electrical signal in an IoT device?
mcqs.explanation: A sensor is specifically designed to detect and measure changes in the environment (e.g., temperature, light) and convert them into a digital or analog signal that the processor can understand.

mcqs.question: What is a key differentiating factor between a Microcontroller Unit (MCU) and a Microprocessor Unit (MPU) in the context of IoT?

options.text: An MPU is always more powerful than an MCU.
An MCU integrates memory and peripherals on a single chip, while an MPU requires external support chips.
An MCU can connect to the internet, while an MPU cannot.
An MPU is used for sensing, while an MCU is used for actuation.

mcqs.explanation: This is the fundamental architectural difference. An MCU is a self-contained system-on-a-chip (SoC), making it simpler and more power-efficient. An MPU is a standalone CPU that relies on external components for functionality.

mcqs.question: For a large-scale agricultural project requiring thousands of soil moisture sensors to send small amounts of data over long distances, which connectivity technology is MOST suitable?
mcqs.explanation: LoRaWAN is a Low-Power Wide-Area Network (LPWAN) protocol designed for long-range communication (several kilometers) with very low power consumption, perfect for battery-operated sensors in remote locations.

mcqs.question: Which layer of the standard IoT architecture model is primarily composed of the physical IoT devices, including their sensors and actuators?
mcqs.explanation: The Perception Layer is the physical foundation of IoT, consisting of devices with sensors and actuators that interact directly with the environment. The Network Layer handles transport, Processing Layer handles analytics, and Application Layer provides user services.

mcqs.question: Why is power management a critical design constraint for most IoT devices?

options.text: To make the devices as heavy as possible.
To reduce the computational speed for better accuracy.
To ensure the device can operate for extended periods, often on a battery, without frequent maintenance.
To increase the cost of the device for higher profit margins.

mcqs.explanation: Many IoT devices are deployed in hard-to-reach locations or in such large numbers that frequent battery replacement is impractical and expensive. Low-power design with sleep modes is essential for practical deployment.

mcqs.question: Which I/O interface protocol uses a two-wire serial bus (SDA and SCL) to connect multiple low-speed peripherals to a microcontroller?
mcqs.explanation: I2C (Inter-Integrated Circuit) uses two wires: SDA (data) and SCL (clock) to connect multiple peripherals on a shared bus. Each device has a unique address. It supports multiple devices on the same two wires, making it efficient for connecting sensors.

mcqs.question: An IoT device must operate for 5 years on a single coin cell battery and transmit 100 bytes of data once per hour from a rural location 10 km from the nearest gateway. Which combination of technologies is most appropriate?
mcqs.explanation: The requirements demand ultra-low power (5-year battery), long range (10 km), and small data (100 bytes/hour). A low-power MCU with deep sleep modes combined with LoRaWAN (long-range, low-power, small payload) is the only option meeting all three constraints.

mcqs.question: Which of the following is NOT a characteristic that distinguishes an IoT device from a general-purpose computer?

options.text: IoT devices typically operate with constrained resources (limited CPU, memory, power)
IoT devices are designed to sense the physical environment
IoT devices always require a keyboard and display for user interaction
IoT devices are designed to operate autonomously without constant human intervention

mcqs.explanation: IoT devices are typically headless (no display/keyboard) and operate autonomously. They interact with the physical world through sensors and actuators, not through traditional human interfaces. Options A, B, and D are genuine characteristics of IoT devices.
